engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 504 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 504 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.3L Diesel
entries.history: Legendary Indenor 2.3L diesel, backbone of 504 diesel range; famed for durability but head gasket and liner seal issues drive replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: GLD, GR Diesel, L Diesel, Pickup, Familiale Diesel
commonFailures: Wet liner seal leaks causing coolant/oil mix
Injector pump wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for XD2 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: Early 2.1L Indenor unit, predecessor to XD2; less common but sought for pre-1979 restorations requiring period-correct engines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for XD90 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Smaller Indenor diesel for early 504s; rare in UK, mostly found in pre-1975 imports and specialist restorations.
entries.cta: Get quotes for XD4x88 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Later 2.5L Indenor unit, sometimes swapped into 504s; more common in 505 but compatible with careful mounting adaptation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for XD3 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Workhorse 2.0L carburettor petrol, most common 504 petrol engine; reliable but timing chain stretch and carb wear drive replacement enquiries.
compatibleVariants: GL, GR, SR, L, Familiale Petrol
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure (70k+ miles)
Solex carburettor wear and fuel delivery issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for XN1 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Injection
entries.history: Fuel-injected evolution of XN1 with Kugelfischer mechanical injection; fitted to TI models, sought for performance restorations.
commonFailures: Kugelfischer injection pump failure or calibration loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for XN2 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Petrol
entries.history: Later 1.9L petrol introduced for 1980s models; simpler than XN series but timing belt failures require vigilant maintenance.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing valve damage if not replaced
Carburettor jet clogging on low-use vehicles
Oil pump wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for XU9 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Early 1.8L petrol for first-generation 504s; now rare, mostly found in 1968-1970 models and enthusiast restorations.
compatibleVariants: Early GL, base saloon, pre-facelift models
commonFailures: Cooling system corrosion leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for XM7 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Smallest petrol option for early 504s; very rare in UK, mostly of interest to concours restorers seeking originality.
compatibleVariants: Base model early saloons, import vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for XC7P Peugeot 504 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Rare PRV V6 fitted exclusively to Pininfarina Cabriolets; ultra-collectible, specialist rebuilds essential due to unique mounting and cooling.
commonFailures: Oil pump wear leading to bearing damage
Cylinder head gasket failure on overheated units
entries.cta: Get quotes for ZMJ Peugeot 504 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 504 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: XD2 Head Gasket Failure - Why Peugeot 504 Diesel Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 504 GLD, GR, Pickup, Familiale (1975-1988, XD2 Indenor 2.3L Diesel)
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping under thermal stress combined with original gasket material degradation; exacerbated by cooling system neglect.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, reseal liners
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix only; head may warp again if not skimmed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removed, machined flat, new gasket, liner seals replaced
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is sound; 40-60k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded gasket, tested liners, bench-run
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage 504s; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: On a classic 504 valued £5k-£15k, a full rebuilt XD2 at £1,800-£2,400 offers better long-term value than repeated gasket repairs. We recommend replacement if the engine has covered over 100k miles or shows liner seal leakage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt XD2 engine prices for your Peugeot 504

problems.h4: XN1 Timing Chain Stretch - Why Peugeot 504 Petrol Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 504 GL, GR, SR, L (1970-1983, XN1 2.0L Petrol)
problems.rootCause: Original timing chain tensioner loses pressure; chain stretches causing valve timing drift, rattling noise, and eventual jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; not viable if chain has jumped

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head to inspect valves, replace chain set, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if no valve damage; requires specialist knowledge

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded chain system, tested components
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage or neglected engines; warranty included

problems.recommendation: If your XN1 is rattling but still runs, a preventative chain replacement at £500-£800 may save the engine. If the chain has jumped or valves are bent, replacement is the only reliable option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for XN1 Peugeot 504 engine replacement

problems.h4: XU9 Timing Belt Failure - Why Peugeot 504 1.9L Engines Stop Suddenly
problems.rootCause: Timing belt snaps due to age, oil contamination, or missed replacement intervals; interference design causes valve-piston collision.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative maintenance only; not a repair after failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, reseat, new belt kit
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block/pistons undamaged; labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with new belt fitted, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution post-failure; warranty included

problems.recommendation: XU9 belt changes every 40k miles or 4 years are non-negotiable. If the belt has snapped, replacement is almost always more economical than valve repair on a 40+ year old engine.

problems.h4: ZMJ V6 Oil Pump Wear - Why Peugeot 504 Cabriolet Engines Lose Pressure
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 504 V6 Cabriolet (1977-1983, ZMJ PRV 2.7L V6)
problems.rootCause: Original oil pump gears wear; low oil pressure leads to bearing knock and eventual crankshaft damage on these rare, high-value engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drop sump, replace oil pump, flush system, refill
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early before bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace main/rod bearings, new pump, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Specialist work; viable for low-mileage, well-maintained V6s

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete PRV V6 rebuild with upgraded pump, balanced assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Essential for valuable Cabriolets; preserves collector value

problems.recommendation: Given the ZMJ's rarity and the Cabriolet's £25k+ value, we recommend full rebuild at first sign of oil pressure drop. Preventative pump replacement on low-mileage examples is wise.
problems.cta: Enquire about ZMJ Peugeot 504 V6 engine availability

problems.h4: Kugelfischer Injection Failure - Why Peugeot 504 TI Engines Run Rough
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 504 TI, SR Injection (1972-1983, XN2 2.0L Petrol Injection)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000+ miles or long periods of inactivity
problems.rootCause: Mechanical Kugelfischer pump seals harden, internal components wear; fuel delivery becomes inconsistent causing rough running or no-start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, specialist bench service, recalibrate, refit
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if pump is serviceable; requires injection specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace with reconditioned pump unit, calibration, test run
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if block/head are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with serviced/new injection system
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage TI models; preserves performance

problems.recommendation: Kugelfischer pumps are specialist items. If your TI runs rough but starts, pump service at £400-£650 may resolve it. For no-start or heavy wear, engine replacement with serviced injection is the reliable route.
problems.cta: Compare XN2 Peugeot 504 TI engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 504
finalCta.paragraph: From XD2 head gaskets to ZMJ oil pumps, Peugeot 504 engines have known failure points - but replacement doesn't mean writing off your classic. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who supply rebuilt units with upgraded components addressing the original weakness. Compare prices, check warranty terms, and arrange supply & fit nationwide - all without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 504 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 504 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.intro: Every Peugeot 504 engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Peugeot 504 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally cleaned, inspected and resealed Peugeot 504 engines with worn parts replaced; ideal for classic restorations on a budget.
types.frontDescription: Professionally cleaned, inspected and resealed Peugeot 504 engines with worn parts replaced; ideal for classic restorations on a budget.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitability depends on core engine condition; warranty terms vary by supplier.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Peugeot 504 engines undergo thorough cleaning, measurement and resealing. Worn bearings, seals and gaskets are replaced. Ideal for XD2, XN1 units with sound blocks.
backBullets: Best value for sound-core classic 504 engines

types.title: Peugeot 504 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive teardown, machining and rebuild of Peugeot 504 engines with upgraded components addressing known failure points like XD2 head gaskets.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive teardown, machining and rebuild of Peugeot 504 engines with upgraded components addressing known failure points like XD2 head gaskets.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code and upgrade specification selected.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Peugeot 504 engines are fully disassembled, machined to tolerance and reassembled with upgraded gaskets, seals and bearings. Addresses XD2 liner seals, XN timing chains.
backBullets: Upgraded components target original failure points
Preferred choice for high-mileage or neglected classics

types.title: Peugeot 504 Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage, tested second-hand Peugeot 504 engines sourced from breakers; budget option for quick replacements where core is beyond repair.

types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and history vary; limited warranty; suitability check essential.
types.backDescription: Used Peugeot 504 engines offer a budget route when speed matters. All units are compression-tested and history-checked where possible. Best for XN1, XD2 with known history.
backBullets: Fastest availability for urgent classic car projects
Always verify compression and service history first
Shorter warranty; ideal for temporary or budget builds

types.title: Peugeot 504 Remanufactured Engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Lead times longer; confirm exact specification with supplier before ordering.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured Peugeot 504 engines meet original factory specifications. Every component is inspected, machined or replaced. Ideal for ZMJ V6 Cabriolet and concours saloon builds.
backBullets: Closest to factory-new specification and reliability
Premium investment for valuable or show-quality classics

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 504 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Peugeot 504 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The smallest diesel option for early 504s, the 1.9L Indenor unit powered base models before 1975; now rare in the UK but sought by restorers aiming for period-correct specifications on pre-facelift saloons.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 504 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 504 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced in 1975 as Peugeot's first mainstream diesel for the 504, the 2.1L Indenor offered improved torque for saloon and estate use; predecessor to the legendary XD2 but less common in UK replacements today.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 504 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 504 2.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the Peugeot 504 diesel range from 1979 onwards, the 2.3L XD2 Indenor is legendary for durability but head gasket and wet liner seal failures drive most UK replacement enquiries for GLD, GR and Pickup variants.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 504 2.3L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 504 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The larger 2.5L Indenor unit, more common in the 505 but sometimes swapped into late 504 Pickups and commercial variants for extra torque; requires mounting adaptation and specialist fitting knowledge.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 504 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 504 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level petrol for the earliest 504 saloons (1968-1970), the 1.6L XC7P is now very rare in the UK; mostly of interest to concours restorers seeking absolute originality on first-generation models.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 504 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 504 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced for the 1969 facelift, the 1.8L XM7 offered a balance of refinement and economy for early GL and base models; now uncommon but valued for pre-1975 restorations requiring period-correct power.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 504 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 504 1.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The later 1.9L XU9 petrol replaced the XN series in final-production 504 saloons (1980-1985); simpler design but timing belt failures require strict maintenance intervals to avoid catastrophic valve damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causing valve damage
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 504 1.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 504 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common Peugeot 504 petrol engine across its production life, the 2.0L XN series (carburettor XN1 and injection XN2) powered GL, GR, SR and TI variants; timing chain stretch and carb/injection wear dominate replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 504 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 504 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: Exclusive to the Pininfarina-designed 504 Cabriolet, the 2.7L ZMJ PRV V6 is ultra-rare (under 1,000 produced) and commands premium value; specialist rebuilds are essential due to unique mounting, cooling and oiling requirements. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot 504 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Peugeot 504 specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump wear leading to bearing damage
head gasket failure on overheated units

items.title: Peugeot 504 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power arrived in the 504 in 1975 with the Indenor family, dominating UK replacement demand due to the legendary XD2's durability and high mileage potential in saloon, estate and pickup variants.
items.descriptor: Diesel power arrived in the 504 in 1975 with the Indenor family, dominating UK replacement demand due to the legendary XD2's durability and high mileage potential in saloon, estate and pickup variants.
families: XD2 - 2.3L workhorse (GLD/GR/Pickup), head gasket & liner seal issues drive replacements
XD90 - 2.1L early diesel (pre-1979), glow plug and injector wear common
XD4x88 - 1.9L rare early unit, mostly import/restoration interest
XD3 - 2.5L later swap option, mounting adaptation required

Head gasket and wet liner seal failures at 80k-120k miles
Strong torque for estate and pickup load-carrying
High UK replacement demand on GLD and GR variants

items.title: Peugeot 504 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol powered the 504 from launch in 1968, with the versatile 2.0L XN series dominating UK registrations; timing chain stretch (XN1) and Kugelfischer injection issues (XN2) are key replacement drivers.
items.descriptor: Petrol powered the 504 from launch in 1968, with the versatile 2.0L XN series dominating UK registrations; timing chain stretch (XN1) and Kugelfischer injection issues (XN2) are key replacement drivers.
families: XN1 - 2.0L carburettor (GL/GR/SR), timing chain stretch at 70k+ miles
XN2 - 2.0L Kugelfischer injection (TI), pump calibration and seal failures
XU9 - 1.9L late-model petrol, timing belt failure risk if neglected
XM7/XC7P - 1.8L/1.6L early units, rare, restoration-focused demand
foundIn: Saloon (1968-1985) - XC7P, XM7, XN1, XN2, XU9
knownFor: XN1 timing chain rattle and stretch is well-documented
XN2 TI injection offers classic performance but specialist maintenance
XU9 belt changes critical every 40k miles to avoid valve damage
Early 1.6L/1.8L units sought for concours originality

items.title: Peugeot 504 Performance & Specialist Engines
items.description: The Pininfarina 504 Cabriolet's exclusive 2.7L ZMJ PRV V6 represents the pinnacle of 504 performance; ultra-rare, collector-valued, and requiring specialist rebuilding knowledge.
items.descriptor: The Pininfarina 504 Cabriolet's exclusive 2.7L ZMJ PRV V6 represents the pinnacle of 504 performance; ultra-rare, collector-valued, and requiring specialist rebuilding knowledge.
families: ZMJ - 2.7L PRV V6 (Cabriolet only), oil pump wear and bearing issues
Mechanical fuel injection with Kugelfischer-derived pump on early units
Unique front-mounted oil cooler and specific mounting points for Cabriolet chassis
importantNotes: Fewer than 1,000 ZMJ-engined Cabriolets produced worldwide
Replacement requires specialist knowledge of V6 mounting and cooling
Availability is extremely limited; rebuilt units command premium pricing

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 504 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major Peugeot 504 engine changes from 1968 launch to 1988 pickup production end, organised so classic car owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Early Launch: XC7P & XM7 Petrol Debut
years.description: The Peugeot 504 launched with carburettor petrol engines only, establishing the classic saloon silhouette that would define the model for two decades.
keyChanges: Model launch with 1.6L XC7P and 1.8L XM7 petrol engines
Rear-wheel drive layout with live rear axle
Pininfarina styling debuts on saloon body
No diesel options available in this period
knownFor: Simple, robust early petrol engines with carburettors
Rust protection less advanced than later models
Highly sought by restorers for originality
Parts availability good for mechanicals, challenging for trim
years.cta: Get quotes for 1968-1970 Peugeot 504 replacement engines

years.description: The introduction of the 2.0L XN1 petrol engine in 1970 created the definitive 504 powertrain, balancing performance and economy for UK buyers.
keyChanges: XN1 2.0L petrol engine introduced, replacing smaller units in most trims
Estate (Familiale) body style added to range
Improved interior trim and equipment levels
Carburettor tuning refinements for emissions
mainEngines: XM7 - 1.8L petrol (base models, early production overlap)
knownFor: XN1 timing chain stretch becomes a known wear item at higher mileage
Strong parts interchangeability across petrol variants
Estate variants popular for family and light commercial use
Early rust issues on sills and arches affect engine bay access
years.cta: Get quotes for 1970-1974 Peugeot 504 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 504 XN1 timing chain replacement • Peugeot 504 GL engine rebuild cost • Peugeot 504 estate diesel conversion

years.description: Peugeot introduced diesel power to the 504 range in 1975, responding to UK fleet and high-mileage driver demand for fuel economy.
keyChanges: First diesel engines offered: 1.9L XD4x88 and 2.1L XD90 Indenor units
GLD trim launched as diesel saloon flagship
Improved sound deadening for diesel NVH
Fuel system updates for cold-weather starting
mainEngines: XD4x88 - 1.9L diesel (base D trim, early production)
knownFor: XD90 glow plug circuit failures common on cold mornings
Diesel variants command premium on classic market today
Indenor engines robust but require regular injector service
Early diesel models lack later XD2 refinements
years.cta: Get quotes for 1975-1978 Peugeot 504 replacement engines

years.description: The legendary XD2 2.3L diesel arrived in 1979, becoming the most common 504 engine in UK replacement enquiries; the fuel-injected XN2 powered the desirable TI performance saloon.
keyChanges: XD2 2.3L Indenor diesel replaces XD90 as standard diesel unit
XN2 2.0L with Kugelfischer mechanical injection launched for TI trim
Cabriolet production peaks with ZMJ V6 option (limited numbers)
Minor facelift updates to lights and interior trim
knownFor: XD2 head gasket and liner seal failures at 80k-120k miles dominate enquiries
XN2 Kugelfischer injection requires specialist calibration knowledge
ZMJ V6 Cabriolet ultra-rare, high-value, specialist rebuild essential
Pickup variants gain popularity for classic utility conversions
years.cta: Get quotes for 1979-1983 Peugeot 504 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 504 XD2 head gasket replacement • Peugeot 504 TI engine rebuild • Peugeot 504 V6 Cabriolet engine for sale
enquiries: Peugeot 504 V6 Cabriolet engine for sale

years.description: As saloon production wound down, the 1.9L XU9 petrol and continued XD2 diesel powered the final 504s, with Pickup production continuing to 1988 for commercial users.
keyChanges: XU9 1.9L petrol introduced for final saloon production (1980-1985)
Saloon production ends in most markets by 1985
Pickup production continues to 1988 for commercial/utility demand
Simplified trim levels and equipment as model approaches end
knownFor: XU9 timing belt critical: failure causes valve damage if not replaced
Final XD2 units benefit from running improvements over early versions
Pickup variants popular for classic restoration and light commercial use
Parts availability remains good for mechanicals, challenging for late-model trim
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984-1988 Peugeot 504 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 504 XU9 timing belt replacement • Peugeot 504 Pickup engine rebuild • Peugeot 504 final year engine compatibility

faq.h2: Peugeot 504 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 504 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 504 engine typically costs £1,600-£2,400 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel XD2 units sit at £1,800-£2,400, while petrol XN1 engines range £1,600-£2,100. Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200.
keyPoints: XD2 diesel rebuilt: £1,800-£2,400 supply-only
XN1 petrol rebuilt: £1,600-£2,100 supply-only
ZMJ V6 Cabriolet: £3,500-£6,000 due to rarity
Supply & fit service adds £600-£1,200 labour
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 504 engine replacement prices now

items.question: Which Peugeot 504 engine is most reliable for classic use?
items.answer: The XD2 2.3L diesel is widely regarded as the most robust Peugeot 504 engine for classic use, offering strong torque and longevity when maintained. The XN1 2.0L petrol is also reliable if timing chain wear is addressed preventatively.
keyPoints: XD2 diesel: legendary durability, watch head gasket/liner seals
XN1 petrol: robust if timing chain replaced preventatively at 70k miles
Avoid neglected units regardless of engine code
Regular oil changes critical for all 504 engines
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Peugeot 504 engine with warranty

items.question: What causes Peugeot 504 XD2 diesel engines to fail?
items.answer: XD2 failures are typically head gasket blowouts or wet liner seal leaks, often triggered by overheating or age-related gasket degradation. These issues commonly appear between 80,000-120,000 miles on original units.
keyPoints: Head gasket failure from thermal stress and material age
Wet liner seal leaks cause coolant/oil contamination
Overheating accelerates both failure modes
Upgraded gaskets in rebuilt units address original weakness
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt XD2 Peugeot 504 engine

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a classic Peugeot 504?
items.answer: Yes, if your 504 has collector value (Cabriolet £25k+, clean saloon £5k-£15k). A rebuilt engine at £1,600-£2,400 preserves value and reliability. For rough examples, compare engine cost against total project budget.
keyPoints: Cabriolet V6: replacement almost always justified by value
Clean saloon/estate: rebuilt engine enhances usability and value
Rough project cars: budget engine cost against full restoration spend
All rebuilt units include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 504 engine prices vs car value

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Peugeot 504?
items.answer: Yes, but compatibility depends on mounting points, transmission match, and cooling layout. XD2 swaps between saloon/estate/pickup are straightforward; V6 or later XU swaps require specialist adaptation.
keyPoints: XD2 fits most diesel 504 variants with minimal modification
XN1/XN2 petrol engines share mounting across petrol variants
ZMJ V6 requires Cabriolet-specific mounts and cooling
Always verify engine code compatibility before ordering
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 504 engine swap compatibility

items.question: How do I identify my Peugeot 504 engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on a plate on the cylinder block or head, often near the dipstick or timing cover. Common codes: XD2 (diesel), XN1/XN2 (petrol), ZMJ (V6). Enter your reg above for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: Check plate on cylinder block/head near dipstick
XD2 = 2.3L diesel; XN1 = 2.0L carb petrol; ZMJ = 2.7L V6
Registration lookup via DVLA confirms exact fitment
Photos of stamping help specialists verify before quoting
items.cta: Get instant Peugeot 504 engine code identification

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Peugeot 504 engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned 504 engines are cleaned, inspected and resealed with worn parts replaced; rebuilt units undergo full teardown, machining and assembly with upgraded components addressing known failure points like XD2 head gaskets.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: cost-effective for sound-core engines
Rebuilt: comprehensive, upgraded components, preferred for high-mileage
Both carry minimum 12-month warranty via EngineMarket network
Rebuilt recommended for engines over 100k miles or with known issues
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Peugeot 504 engine options

items.question: Do Peugeot 504 engine specialists offer supply and fit?
items.answer: Yes, many UK specialists in the EngineMarket network offer supply & fit for Peugeot 504 engines, handling delivery, removal, fitting, fluids and commissioning - essential for owners without classic car workshop access.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 to engine price typically
Specialists understand 504-specific mounting and cooling quirks
Warranty can cover parts and labour when arranged together
Nationwide network covers most UK locations
items.cta: Find Peugeot 504 engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 504 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch in 2-4 weeks after order confirmation. Full supply & fit jobs usually complete in 3-7 working days once the engine arrives at the fitting garage, depending on variant complexity.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine build time: 2-4 weeks average
Supply & fit installation: 3-7 working days typical
ZMJ V6 Cabriolet may require longer due to specialist handling
Delays possible for rare cores or high-demand periods
items.cta: Get a timeline quote for your Peugeot 504 engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with Peugeot 504 replacement engines?
items.answer: Every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Peugeot 504 engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary slightly by supplier - always confirm before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on network rebuilt units
Unlimited mileage cover on most specialist-supplied engines
Warranty covers parts and labour if supply & fit arranged
Always get warranty terms in writing before payment
items.cta: Check Peugeot 504 engine warranty terms before you buy
